title: Polestar global sales up 13% in Q3 as it exits China retail network
text: Polestar exited its China retail network while achieving 13% sales growth globally in Q3. The company closed 26 of its 36 stores in China, a significant retreat from the country where Polestar initially hoped to establish strong market presence. Sales in China totaled only 3,120 cars in 2024, representing just 7% of worldwide sales. The withdrawal reflects intensifying competition from dominant local brands like BYD, Xiaomi, Xpeng, and Li Auto. European sales demonstrated much stronger momentum, growing 76% year-over-year. The company now focuses retail expansion on Western European markets including the UK, France, and Germany, with plans to enter Eastern European markets from 2026 onwards. This geographic pivot represents a strategic repositioning away from China-centric growth toward European dominance. The shift also supports Polestar's localized manufacturing strategy, moving production away from China to South Korea and Europe. By redirecting resources from underperforming China retail to flourishing European markets, Polestar aims to achieve sustainable sales growth and improve margins through regional production efficiency.
